channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Treasurer Joey Spellerberg on First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome Nebraska State Treasurer Joey Spellerberg. During the episode Treasurer Spellerberg and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss LB 938, which creates First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska, the role of the Treasurer’s Office, and solutions for Nebraska’s high property taxes. Listen now on Apple...

The post Nebraskanomics: Treasurer Joey Spellerberg on First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] The Fraud that Wasn’t: Audit Shows Arizona ESA Program 98% Strong
item.description:
By Kathryn Hickok Recent news reporting in Arizona claimed that more than 20...

The post The Fraud that Wasn’t: Audit Shows Arizona ESA Program 98% Strong appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] ExoticRidge v. Hawkins County: Fighting back against an arbitrary data center ban in East Tennessee
item.description: Executive Summary Technology is evolving like never before. Americans today benefit from rapid advancements in fields like artificial intelligence. These cloud-based advancements have a physical component: data centers. The data center industry has already generated trillions of dollars for the U.S. economy. And with companies projecting trillions of dollars more in investments to keep up...

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Jim Vokal Rants about Property Taxes
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as host Jim Vokal rants about rising property taxes, the shell game elected officials are playing, and solutions Platte is advocating for this year. Listen now on Apple Podcasts and Spotify.

The post Nebraskanomics: Jim Vokal Rants about Property Taxes appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] TriMet’s $320M Painful Plan to Gridlock 82nd Avenue
item.description:
by Randal O’Toole Bus rapid transit was originally supposed to provide light-rail-like services...

The post TriMet’s $320M Painful Plan to Gridlock 82nd Avenue appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] From Bad to Worse: Augusta Adds Income Tax Hike to an Already Flawed Supplemental Budget
item.description:
Over the last several months, Maine Policy Institute has repeatedly pointed out the many problems that exist in Governor Mills’ 2026 supplemental budget. Maine lawmakers had an opportunity to fix serious flaws in the budget. Instead, the Legislature’s Appropriations Committee has taken an already problematic proposal from Janet Mills, doubled down, and made it worse. […]

The post From Bad to Worse: Augusta Adds Income Tax Hike to an Already Flawed Supplemental Budget appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: Ranked Choice Voting the Wrong Approach
item.description:
The following appeared in the Albuquerque Journal on April 4, 2026 Albuquerque’s “progressives” are at it again. Ever since Republican RJ Berry won a three-way election for mayor in 2009 (against two Democrats), Albuquerque has had runoff elections requiring candidates to achieve more than 50% of the vote. These runoffs can be costly. The city […]

The post Opinion piece: Ranked Choice Voting the Wrong Approach appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: Curley effect is keeping N.M. stagnant
item.description:
The following appeared on April 5, 2026 in the Santa Fe New Mexican. The article also appeared in papers across New Mexico. I recently came across a concept called the Curley Effect. It has nothing to do with Larry and Moe. After some research, I learned the Curley Effect, coined by economists Edward L. Glaeser […]

The post Opinion piece: Curley effect is keeping N.M. stagnant appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Mspolicy] The Southern Resurgence - What the Numbers Tell Us
item.description:
Something significant is happening in America's South, and it deserves more attention. While New York and California are losing residents, states like South Carolina and Alabama are gaining population at a record pace - and alongside that growth, it is southern states like ours that are generating some of the most impressive economic numbers in the […]

The post The Southern Resurgence - What the Numbers Tell Us appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Foropportunity] GCO Praises Federal Leaders for Empowering States to Strengthen Pathways to Work through the Stronger Workforce for America Act
item.description:
The Stronger Workforce for America Act of 2026 includes an invaluable option for state flexibility, the Make America Skilled Again Grants. Georgia and other states need this option so they can design workforce and safety net systems that meet families' immediate needs while providing a clearer path to opportunity and self-sufficiency.

The post GCO Praises Federal Leaders for Empowering States to Strengthen Pathways to Work through the Stronger Workforce for America Act appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] WASHINGTON: It’s about giving workers options
item.description:
Public sector unions are not just workplace representatives; they’re powerful political actors with influence that extends across ballot initiatives, elected officials, nonprofit organizations and super PACs. 

The post WASHINGTON: It’s about giving workers options appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Beacontn] Energy Reliability is Only as Strong as its Weakest Wire
item.description: The Volunteer State is building a serious energy future that will continue to anchor our leadership in the sector. Allowing competition and technology to improve our state's grid will not only make the state more attractive for investment but also keep more Tennesseans safe when it matters most.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] AEA leaves members in the dark as Alabama Policy Institute, Freedom Foundation weigh in on payroll deduction ban bill
item.description:
Every week during the legislative session, the Alabama Education Association (AEA) sends its members The Capitol Pulse, highlighting its priorities and informing them about pending, filed and moving legislation impacting teachers.

The post AEA leaves members in the dark as Alabama Policy Institute, Freedom Foundation weigh in on payroll deduction ban bill appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: William Beard on how Nebraska Competes for Data Centers
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome William Beard, co-author of the Goldwater Institute’s report: Data Centers a Free Market Model for the Digital Future. During the episode William and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss what data centers are, why they matter, and what separates the states that are winning investment...

The post Nebraskanomics: William Beard on how Nebraska Competes for Data Centers appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Maine’s Law Court Gets it Right: RCV Violates Maine Constitution
item.description:
The Maine Supreme Judicial Court has issued a unanimous advisory opinion in which the Court concluded that the Legislature’s attempt to expand ranked-choice voting (RCV) to general elections for governor, state senator, and state representative would violate the Maine Constitution.  Although the decision came quickly, the outcome closely tracks the concerns that surfaced during oral […]

The post Maine’s Law Court Gets it Right: RCV Violates Maine Constitution appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] CALIFORNIA: Union hubris in any language
item.description:
After Freedom Foundation’s National Canvassing Director Dustin James reported that Spanish-speaking union represented workers were being taken advantage of, I knew I had to go down and see it for myself.

The post CALIFORNIA: Union hubris in any language appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Idaho’s Budget Grew 2.5% Despite the Cuts, because of Welfare Spending
item.description: If you were paying attention to the budget debates on both the House and Senate floors during the legislative session, you might think the Legislature made significant cuts to the Fiscal Year 2027 (FY27) budget. Unfortunately, based on preliminary numbers, total appropriations are a 2.5% increase, with FY27’s $14.453 billion outpacing the FY26 original appropriation […]

item.title: [Jbartlett] SNAP candy & soft drink ban would hurt retailers
item.description:
Removing candy and soft drinks from New Hampshire’s SNAP program would cost retailers an estimated $5.3 million-$17.8 million a year in lost candy and soft drink sales alone, a new study by the Josiah Bartlett Center for Public Policy finds. The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services and the U.S. Department of Agriculture have […]

The post SNAP candy & soft drink ban would hurt retailers appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] How a Levy Cap Works and Why Florida Needs One
item.description:
Florida property taxes now exceed $55 billion per year — the single largest source of local government revenue....

The post How a Levy Cap Works and Why Florida Needs One appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] Don’t Tax the World Cup, How New Jersey Could Get This Wrong
item.description:
By Wells Winegar New Jersey is about to host one of the biggest global events in the world. The FIFA […]

The post Don’t Tax the World Cup, How New Jersey Could Get This Wrong first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Wisconsin’s southern border shows what freedom brings
item.description:
From 2011 to 2024, Wisconsin counties beat their Illinois counterparts 103 percent to 68 percent in private-sector economic output.

The post Wisconsin’s southern border shows what freedom brings appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Legislative Session Wrap Up: Progress, Setbacks, and What’s Next
item.description:
The 2026 legislative session has concluded. Lawmakers could return to address final items, such as veto overrides, but the session is effectively over. As we reflect on the last few months, one word comes to mind to describe this legislative session: disappointing. We will likely remember this session more for what didn’t get done than…

The post 2026 Legislative Session Wrap Up: Progress, Setbacks, and What’s Next first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] A Step in the Right Direction on Energy, Now Let’s Finish the Job
item.description:
by Wells Winegar New Jersey just made a long overdue move. With Governor Mikie Sherrill signing legislation to lift the […]

The post A Step in the Right Direction on Energy, Now Let’s Finish the Job first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] John Charles Submits Public Comments on ODOE’s “TIGHGER 2.0” Climate Gap Measures
item.description:
By John A. Charles, Jr. The Oregon Department of Energy and the Oregon...

The post John Charles Submits Public Comments on ODOE’s “TIGHGER 2.0” Climate Gap Measures appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Idaho ends taxpayer support for teachers’ unions
item.description:
Idaho Gov. Brad Little on April 10 signed into law a Freedom Foundation-supported measure crafted to end taxpayer support for teachers' unions in the Gem State. 

The post Idaho ends taxpayer support for teachers’ unions appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Beacontn] Bethlehem Manor LLC v. City of Bethlehem: Fighting back against immunity for arbitrary zoning decisions at the Supreme Court
item.description: Beacon is fighting back against immunity for arbitrary zoning decisions at the Supreme Court.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansans Need a Responsible Budget
item.description: The Kansas House and Senate have already passed the FY 2027 budget bill, House Bill 2513, and sent it to Governor Laura Kelly. Aside from some line-item vetoes and (failed) overrides, the topline is essentially the same. Supporters are calling it responsible because it trims the State General Fund budget by $189.2 million below a […]

item.title: [Empowerms] U.S. Senator Roger Wicker: Strength Abroad, Opportunity at Home
item.description:
U.S. Senator Roger Wicker: Strength Abroad, Opportunity at Home In a time of growing global instability, the role of American strength is back at the center of the conversation. In this episode, Grant sits down with Roger Wicker, Chairman of the Senate Armed Services Committee and Mississippi’s senior senator. From rebuilding America’s military strength to…

The post U.S. Senator Roger Wicker: Strength Abroad, Opportunity at Home first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Will Law] WI Regulation Violates New SCOTUS Precedent Regarding Talk Therapy
item.description:
Wisconsin Family Action, and WILL’s client Joy Buchman are warning Governor Tony Evers and his Administration to immediately cease any enforcement of Wis. Admin. Code. § MPSW 20.02(25), a rule banning consensual, client-driven talk therapy from a perspective the government disfavors. The rule constitutes viewpoint discrimination, similar to the Colorado law recently struck down in Chiles v. Salazar.

The post WI Regulation Violates New SCOTUS Precedent Regarding Talk Therapy appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] Why New Jersey’s Small Businesses Deserve Better Policy
item.description:
by Megan Whitman, NJPI Jr. Fellow Walk down any main street in New Jersey and you’re looking at the heart […]

The post Why New Jersey’s Small Businesses Deserve Better Policy first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] Your Property Tax Bill in 2030
item.description:
By 2030, many Wisconsin taxpayers could face thousands in new taxes, with costs falling heaviest on local taxpayers even under the most optimistic assumptions about new state aid. The interactive calculator we released makes these impacts concrete. Policymakers and voters should carefully weigh whether this path truly serves students and communities or simply expands government at the expense of hardworking Wisconsin families. 

The post Your Property Tax Bill in 2030 appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Mass refugees have brought $6 billion in personal income to NH since 2012
item.description:
Movers from Massachusetts to New Hampshire brought with them $5.95 billion in net adjusted gross income (AGI) from 2012-2023, IRS data show, representing a massive transfer of wealth from the high-tax Bay State to low-tax New Hampshire.  New Hampshire’s net personal income gain from Massachusetts movers grew tenfold from 2012-2019, then suddenly doubled to a […]

The post Mass refugees have brought $6 billion in personal income to NH since 2012 appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] New Study Calls for Reducing or Eliminating Parking Requirements for New Housing
item.description:
Data show the requirements increase rents, reduce housing development 

The post New Study Calls for Reducing or Eliminating Parking Requirements for New Housing appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama lawmakers deliver 29 of 30 conservative victories from API’s ‘record-breaking’ 2026 agenda
item.description:
The Alabama Policy Institute on Monday declared the 2026 regular session its most productive on record.

The post Alabama lawmakers deliver 29 of 30 conservative victories from API’s ‘record-breaking’ 2026 agenda appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Tax Day: A Reflection on State Income Taxes and Competitiveness
item.description:
April 15th is more than a filing deadline – it’s a moment to reflect on how tax policy shapes the economic trajectory of Massachusetts. As residents finalize their returns, a broader question looms: are current tax policies helping the state grow, or quietly pushing people and capital...

The post Tax Day: A Reflection on State Income Taxes and Competitiveness  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] AI & the Liberty Movement
item.description:
Amazon has just announced another multi-billion-dollar data center project in Mississippi — the latest in a flood of inward investment now pouring into our state. But here’s the thing worth reflecting on: even AI-related investments on this scale are only a fraction of what is flowing into data centers and AI infrastructure across the country. […]

The post AI & the Liberty Movement  appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Rifreedom] Law Centre Calls on Rhode Island Department of Health to Stop Enforcing Conversion Therapy Ban After Supreme Court Ruling
item.description:
The Law Centre today delivered a formal letter to Dr. Jerome Larkin, Director of the Rhode Island Department of Health (RIDOH), demanding that the agency immediately cease enforcement of Rhode Island’s ban on conversion therapy for minors. The action follows the U.S. Supreme Court’s March 21, 2026 decision in Chiles v. Salazar, which struck down Colorado’s similar law as a violation of the First Amendment.

The post Law Centre Calls on Rhode Island Department of Health to Stop Enforcing Conversion Therapy Ban After Supreme Court Ruling appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Measure what matters: family structure and its impact on learning
item.description:
In 1969, nearly nine out of 10 American children lived in households with two married parents. By 2023, that figure had fallen to roughly six in 10.

The post Measure what matters: family structure and its impact on learning appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] The SAVE Act: What It Is, Why It Matters, and What Florida’s Experience Tells Us
item.description:
Natalie Gonzalez Election policy is once again at the epicenter of national conversations. Over the past several presidential...

The post The SAVE Act: What It Is, Why It Matters, and What Florida’s Experience Tells Us appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Kneecapping Conservatives, Protecting Bureaucrats: Vander Woude’s Lame Duck Legacy
item.description: As the 2026 legislative session reached its disappointing end, so too did Chairman John Vander Woude’s tenure in the Legislature. Rather than attempting to solidify his legacy as one of defense for children, families, and freedom, he seemingly spent his final days in the Capitol running interference for the Idaho Department of Health and Welfare, […]

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] 2026 Green Book
item.description: 2026 Kansas Green Book – Exploring the relationship between the size of government and economic growth

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Referendums on development could kill state’s growth
item.description:
Subjecting big development proposals to popular vote risks killing statewide economic growth, observers say in the wake of a successful effort by Port Washington data center opponents to give citizens the ability to nix the future use of a key financing tool.

The post Referendums on development could kill state’s growth appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Republican lawmakers say Wisconsin DPI violated open meetings law with Dells trip
item.description:
Republican lawmakers say Wisconsin DPI violated open meetings law with Dells trip DPI says education session included teachers, private vendor per state statute By Corrinne Hess April 15, 2026 New testing standards were rolled out for Wisconsin students in 2024. Shortly after, educators from private, public and charter schools across the state met in the Wisconsin Dells for a […]

The post Republican lawmakers say Wisconsin DPI violated open meetings law with Dells trip appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Benjamin Yount covers the GOAT Committee Hearing on DPI Water Park Weekend
item.description:
Benjamin Yount covers the GOAT Committee Hearing on DPI Water Park Weekend  

The post Benjamin Yount covers the GOAT Committee Hearing on DPI Water Park Weekend appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] Minnesota Parents, We’re About to Lose Out on $800 Million for Our Children
item.description:
By Zach Freimark and Sarah Hitchcock America First Policy Institute On July 4, 2025, Congress passed, on a bipartisan vote, a package of reconciliations, commonly referred to as the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. Included in the bill was a provision that created the Education Freedom Tax Credit (EFTC), which funds elementary and secondary education […]

The post Minnesota Parents, We’re About to Lose Out on $800 Million for Our Children appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Outraged by $4 Gas, Albany Pushes Policies That Mean $6
item.description:
New Yorkers could soon be paying $6 a gallon for gas, thanks to Albany’s policy mistakes. The prospect of soaring....

The post Outraged by $4 Gas, Albany Pushes Policies That Mean $6 appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Build It and They Will Come A FEW OF US have spent considerable time trying to find a simple illustration of how the Indiana Economic Development Corporation (IEDC) works.  Are they trying to mimic a bank in some sloppy way or a Chamber of Commerce? More specifically, how is it a good idea to leverage government money to Read the full article...

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Sine Die
item.description:
Capitol Chat: Sine Die Lawmakers returned to Jackson expecting veto overrides, but what followed was one of the more unusual endings to a legislative session in recent memory. We unpack the final day’s twists, the political fallout, and close the book on the 2026 session.

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Sine Die first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Fact Check: Idaho didn’t ban unions - it just stopped doing their paperwork
item.description: House Bill 516 does not ban unions. It does not prevent teachers from joining them. It does not end collective bargaining. Even critics admit as much. What it does is far simpler — it stops government from acting as the middleman for union operations.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Understanding spending and its growth under Gov. MLG
item.description:
A policy blogger named Maria Davidson recently started posting a series of charts contrasting the growth in state spending vs the growth in the same state’s population over the past decade (one for California is linked). I asked for a New Mexico version of her chart, but none was forthcoming, so I made my own. But then […]

The post Understanding spending and its growth under Gov. MLG appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] 10 Takeaways from a Massive DPI Oversight Hearing
item.description:
10 Takeaways from a Massive DPI Oversight Hearing

The post 10 Takeaways from a Massive DPI Oversight Hearing appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] LEGAL ANALYSIS: U.S. Supreme Court Ruling in Landmark Louisiana Coastal Lawsuit
item.description:
Yesterday, the U.S. Supreme Court issued an 8-0 decision in Chevron USA, Inc. v. Plaquemines Parish. The landmark ruling reaffirmed the rule of law and wisely confirmed that the federal court system is the appropriate forum for the claims made by the State of Louisiana and coastal parishes against Chevron over critical production activities carried...

The post LEGAL ANALYSIS: U.S. Supreme Court Ruling in Landmark Louisiana Coastal Lawsuit appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Empirecenter] New York’s Unhealthy Dependence on Low-Wage Health Care Jobs
item.description:
Two recently published charts tell an eye-opening story about New York City’s economy: The first, found in a report from....

The post New York’s Unhealthy Dependence on Low-Wage Health Care Jobs appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Sacramento’s Decarbonization Mandates Punish Working Families
item.description:
Over the last ten years, residential electricity rates in California have nearly doubled, and the average California household now pays nearly 15% more than the national average on their monthly electricity bills, according to the Energy Information Administration. A key driver behind this is state climate policy and decarbonization mandates. Specifically, in 2015, the legislature...

The post Sacramento’s Decarbonization Mandates Punish Working Families first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] “Tax Me More” Fund Not Seeing Much Action Despite Tax Hikers’ False Appeals to Patriotism
item.description:
Americans are allowed to voluntarily contribute money to the U.S. Treasury’s “tax me more” fund. But not much money is being donated despite the false appeals to “patriotism” peddled by tax hikers. In calling for tax increases, affluent Democrats and progressive activists try to assert that Americans opposed to such tax hikes are lacking in […]

The post “Tax Me More” Fund Not Seeing Much Action Despite Tax Hikers’ False Appeals to Patriotism appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Guardrails for Government Will Work
item.description:
Property tax reform can feel like fighting a war on a thousand fronts. Should the focus be on assessments, levy rates, or rollback? Or on Tax Increment Financing (TIF), targeted credits, and exemptions? School funding adds yet another layer. While all these factors influence property taxes, they often distract from the core issue that has undermined past reform efforts. That issue is government spending.

The post Guardrails for Government Will Work appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] ‘Let me pretend this is true, OK?’
item.description: You’ll find it everywhere in politics when you see the pattern

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Legislators should make transparency a top priority in 2027
item.description: Wyoming legislators should make strengthening the Public Records Act and other government transparency initiatives their center of attention in next year’s legislative session. This would build on the progress made this session for medical cost transparency.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Louisiana Lawmakers Consider Fix for Private School Pre-K Overreach
item.description:
Schools decry largest expansion of government regulation in state’s history, possible closures  In response to months of panicked, angry phone calls from school leaders and families, Louisiana lawmakers are considering bills to dial back sweeping, costly government overreach on private school prekindergarten (pre-K) programs imposed via a new state law.  Act 409 was passed by...

The post Louisiana Lawmakers Consider Fix for Private School Pre-K Overreach appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Secretary of State Bellows Butchers Another Ballot Question
item.description:
In the fall, Maine voters will have the opportunity to vote on a citizen referendum aimed at protecting girls’ sports by requiring schools to separate athletic teams and certain facilities based on biological sex as recorded at birth. Before they ever get the chance to vote on the policy, however, there is a critically important […]

The post Secretary of State Bellows Butchers Another Ballot Question appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Mississippi Overtakes Britain
item.description:
Something remarkable has happened here in Mississippi. The state that for most of the last century sat at the bottom of nearly every American economic table has, quietly, pulled ahead of the United Kingdom in GDP per capita. Last week, Governor Tate Reeves highlighted the fact on X/Twitter in his characteristically Southern style - and […]

The post Mississippi Overtakes Britain appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] $39 Trillion and Counting—States Must Prepare
item.description:
Federal spending and the national debt are on an unsustainable path, with rising deficits and interest costs forcing cuts that are already beginning to impact states. The states, including Iowa, are highly exposed, relying on billions in federal funds that can be reduced, restricted, or eliminated with little warning. Lawmakers can prepare by increasing transparency, oversight, and long-term planning around federal funds—treating them as obligations, not “free money.” The national debt is out of hand, recently surpassing $39 trillion, yet it is largely ignored. While we might notice symptoms, like longer TSA lines at the airport, we often overlook the cause: federal spending and the national debt are on an unsustainable trajectory. The resulting fiscal pressure is already leading to cuts that are likely to accelerate. That reality leaves states like Iowa, which are heavily reliant on federal funds, highly exposed and in need of preparation for what comes next.

The post $39 Trillion and Counting—States Must Prepare appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansas Has a Cost Problem—And It’s Driving People Away
item.description: Kansas isn’t collapsing. That would at least get attention. What’s happening is quieter and more dangerous: the state is becoming too expensive, too fragmented, and too average to compete, while other states pull ahead. The new 2026 Kansas Green Book lays it out clearly. Kansas is not leading where it matters. It is lagging, and […]

item.title: [Mackinac] Freedom notes U.S.A.: Nebraska takes over federal environmental reviews
item.description: Californians burned on electric rates; prices spike in a New York minute

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Road to Freedom – Unravelling the Riddle of David Allen Funston
item.description:
California’s “elderly parole” system, created under AB 3234 and related statutory reforms, allows incarcerated people to be considered for release once they reach age 50 and have served at least 20 years. It is routinely described as a compassionate mechanism for aging inmates and prison population management. But the label “elderly parole” is misleading. Fifty […]

The post Road to Freedom – Unravelling the Riddle of David Allen Funston appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] University of Michigan Conceals DEI Despite Promises of Reform
item.description:
In March 2025, the University of Michigan loudly proclaimed that it would eliminate offices and programs dedicated to “diversity, equity, and inclusion” (DEI). After spending a quarter of..

The post University of Michigan Conceals DEI Despite Promises of Reform  appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Gaski: Save the SAVE Act, Not the Filibuster
item.description: by John F. Gaski, Ph.D. On the prospect of ditching the Senate filibuster in order to pass the SAVE Act, Majority Leader John Thune has been heard to say, “The Democrats may intend to steal the car, but we don’t have to give them the keys.” Translated: Don’t provide future Dems with the filibuster weapon. Read the full article...

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Adds New Deputy Counsel, Expands Litigation Capacity
item.description:
WILL is proud to announce the addition of Rebecca Furdek as Deputy Counsel. Her hire reflects WILL’s continued growth and commitment to litigating high-stakes cases promoting free speech, economic liberty, and equal rights.

The post WILL Adds New Deputy Counsel, Expands Litigation Capacity appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Freedomfoundationofminnesota] Why Walz’s proposed social media tax is a bad idea
item.description:
It would run afoul of the First Amendment, among other issues. By Annette Meeks Guest contributor to the Minnesota Star Tribune April 20, 2026 ••• What do progressives do when they run out of other people’s money to spend? You don’t have to look any further than Minnesota to find out: Recently Gov. Tim Walz […]

The post Why Walz’s proposed social media tax is a bad idea appeared first on Freedom Foundation of Minnesota.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Connecticut Lawmakers Move to Change the Rules While Cases Are Still Pending
item.description:
Connecticut lawmakers are advancing legislation this session that raises a fundamental question about governance: what happens when the state changes the law while courts are still deciding whether it followed […]

The post Connecticut Lawmakers Move to Change the Rules While Cases Are Still Pending  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Do not increase property tax rates on certain homes
item.description:
April 20, 2026, 9 a.m. Kalana O Maui Building To: Maui County Council       Alice Lee, Chair       Yuki Lei Sugimura, Vice Chair From: Jonathan Helton, Policy Analyst            Grassroot Institute of Hawaii RE: REAL PROPERTY TAX RATES FOR FISCAL YEAR 2026-2027 Aloha Chair Lee, Vice Chair […]

The post Do not increase property tax rates on certain homes appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] ‘Millionaires tax’ would hike rate 61% on 22K Illinois small businesses
item.description:
Combined state and federal taxes would take more than 50% of the top-end income for 22,000 Illinois small businesses.

The post ‘Millionaires tax’ would hike rate 61% on 22K Illinois small businesses appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Over Five Years Later, Arbeeny Family Support Still Critical to Promoting Government Transparency
item.description:
Albany, NY — The Arbeeny family and the Empire Center are marking five years of partnership in remembrance of Norman....

The post Over Five Years Later, Arbeeny Family Support Still Critical to Promoting Government Transparency appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Does San Francisco Need Another Union-Backed Tax?
item.description:
San Francisco and Portland are the only two US cities that tax companies if the pay ratio between the CEO and other employees is “too high.”. After agreeing to lower this tax in 2024 via Proposition M, the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) and its organized labor allies are pushing a ballot measure to raise...

The post Does San Francisco Need Another Union-Backed Tax? first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] May 2026’s Top 10 Most Expensive School Bonds
item.description:
Next month, independent school districts (ISDs) will, again, ask voters to approve massive new borrowing schemes that threaten to unleash a wave of tax hikes and bigger bureaucracies.   These fiscal excesses appear widespread too.   Taxpayers in nearly 60 different counties will decide on one or more of the 109 individual propositions up for a vote this election cycle, according to the Texas Bond Review Board’s (BRB’s) bond election database. If these measures are entirely successful,...

The post May 2026’s Top 10 Most Expensive School Bonds first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Inside Texas Universities’ Ties to Iran’s Nuclear Research Network
item.description:
Every Friday at Tehran University, thousands gather on campus for prayer. When the sermon ends, the chants begin: “Death to America.” And yet, Texas universities maintain active academic partnerships with that same institution and its affiliated network. The question Americans should be asking is not whether Iran wants nuclear weapons — it does — but...

The post Inside Texas Universities’ Ties to Iran’s Nuclear Research Network first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Eichenberger: Christian Nationalism
item.description: by Dan Eichenberger, MD, MBA “Christian Nationalism” has become one of the most abused phrases in American political debate. Once an academic term, it is now routinely deployed to delegitimize citizens who believe America’s Judeo-Christian moral inheritance should continue to inform public life. The phrase is rarely defined with precision and seldom applied consistently. Its Read the full article...

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Victory! Arizona Court Strikes Down Water Bureaucracy’s Restrictions on Development
item.description:
An Arizona trial court this afternoon struck down the Arizona Department of Water Resources’ new restriction on development known as..

The post Victory! Arizona Court Strikes Down Water Bureaucracy’s Restrictions on Development appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] They block grocery stores—then build their own at four times the cost
item.description: To be clear, not every community will attract a grocery store overnight, even with fewer barriers. There are places where the economics are genuinely difficult. But that makes it all the more important not to sabotage the projects that are viable.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Agricultural property tax reform would help farmers expand

item.title: [Johnlocke] Media bias group relies on former Iranian lackey
item.description:
Alana Goodman writes for the Washington Free Beacon about a media bias group’s interesting personnel choice. A former paid scribe for an Iranian state-affiliated newspaper now works for a U.S. media watchdog group known for producing a “media bias” chart that rates liberal outlets as more reliable than conservative ones. Meisam Zamanabadi, an analyst with...

The post Media bias group relies on former Iranian lackey appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Alito labeled ‘courageous’ SCOTUS justice
item.description:
Mollie Hemingway writes for the Federalist about her favorite US Supreme Court justice. Justice Samuel Alito marks his 20th year on the Supreme Court, a milestone that coincides with the release of my new book this month, Alito: The Justice Who Reshaped the Supreme Court and Restored the Constitution. The timing feels right. America needs...

The post Alito labeled ‘courageous’ SCOTUS justice appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Left wages war on comedy that’s no laughing matter
item.description:
Armando Simon writes for the American Thinker about one of the political left’s most prominent targets. A curious characteristic of totalitarian communist regimes is the dearth, if not the total absence, or comedy. No comedy films were ever made in the Soviet Union or in any of the other communist countries. In fact, cinemas in...

The post Left wages war on comedy that’s no laughing matter appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] Reform Michigan unemployment insurance
item.description: Current system misses the mark with bad incentives, tax hits during hard times

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Proposed $10 billion housing bond will only burden California cities
item.description:
California’s housing overhaul has been so extensive — 45 bills signed last October alone by Gov. Gavin Newsom — the results won’t be clear for years. Even so, voters this November 3 may get to decide on another $10 billion in housing bonds. That’s from the Affordable Housing Bond Act of 2026, Assembly Bill 736, […]

The post Proposed $10 billion housing bond will only burden California cities appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Restricting Use, Reducing Supply: The Consequences of STR Bans for ADU Production
item.description:
Florida’s housing affordability crisis is no longer theoretical. Population growth continues to outpace homebuilding in Florida, while higher...

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] School Board’s Policy Barring ‘Negative’ Comments is Unconstitutional
item.description:
Parents and community members who attend a Mesa, Ariz., school board meeting can lavish board members, teachers, and staff with..

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Signing Blank Checks with EpiPens
item.description:
I prescribed EpiPens yesterday, knowing they are overpriced. But I was shocked by how much. This EpiPen I prescribed is for a mother who watched her 11-year-old son break out in hives from an insect bite on his arm. The hives then spread over his body. This time, fortunately, the hives did not spread to...

The post Signing Blank Checks with EpiPens first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] IRS: Illinois led nation in rate of income loss in 2023
item.description:
The state lost a bigger share per resident moving out than anywhere else in the country, according to IRS data.

The post IRS: Illinois led nation in rate of income loss in 2023 appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Taxpayers Reject Another Property Tax Hike
item.description:
Property tax frustration is growing nationwide, and taxpayers are increasingly rejecting higher taxes while calling for stronger protections. Massachusetts’ Proposition 2½ shows how effective guardrails can work, as voters in South Hadley rejected major tax increases that could have raised bills by up to 50%. The message is clear: taxpayers expect governments to control spending and prioritize budgets, not simply raise taxes to sustain growth.

The post Taxpayers Reject Another Property Tax Hike appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] The Measure of a Man
item.description: House Bill 461, introduced late in the 2025 legislative session, would have required the state to run an analysis of the Idaho Launch program to determine its effectiveness. I won’t bore you with all the details, but essentially the question is: “Does this program help the state the way Gov. Brad Little and other proponents […]

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] President Trump, Speaker Johnson, National Leaders, API Board & Staff to read passages at America Reads the Bible Event in DC
item.description:
For 84 hours over 8 days almost 500 national leaders will read the entire bible from start to finish.

The post President Trump, Speaker Johnson, National Leaders, API Board & Staff to read passages at America Reads the Bible Event in DC appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] New Rankings Reinforce Connecticut’s Decades-Long Affordability Problem
item.description:
As the legislative session approaches its May 6 conclusion, a new national report underscores what many residents already feel: the state’s affordability challenges remain deeply entrenched.   Despite acknowledging cost reduction as a top priority heading into the […]

The post New Rankings Reinforce Connecticut’s Decades-Long Affordability Problem appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] The Abundance Alliance
item.description:
Abundance, and its political twin, affordability, are now bipartisan mantras, but cannot be realized if the only permissible avenues are via urban infill, renewable energy, and water rationing. California is uniquely positioned to do much more. Breakthrough technologies and big projects, both pioneered here, could unite a powerful coalition of farmers, energy companies, high tech...

The post The Abundance Alliance first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] World Tour: Age Verification Edition
item.description:
Attempts to age gate the internet and restrict social media use based on age checks are not distinct to American state legislatures. Countries with less robust free speech protections have had an easier time passing these regulations at a large scale—with disastrous results. As it turns out, requiring all social media users in a nation...

The post World Tour: Age Verification Edition appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Kansas City Takes Steps Toward Better Housing Policy
item.description:
Kansas City has made some meaningful changes to how it regulates housing development, and they are worth applauding. In recent weeks, city leaders have advanced reforms that begin to reduce […]

The post Kansas City Takes Steps Toward Better Housing Policy appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] County commissioner’s ill-conceived comments over sheriff’s role in public safety roundly criticized
item.description:
Back in March, Hennepin County commissioner Marion Greene stated during a board meeting that, “I’m going to reveal my cards, I’m not willing to fund the sheriff to provide public safety for Hennepin County. I don’t think that is something that office can take on and do well.” The statement understandably drew a great deal...

The post County commissioner’s ill-conceived comments over sheriff’s role in public safety roundly criticized  appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Democrats Didn’t Discover The Insurance Crisis. They Created It
item.description:
Sen. Ron Wyden, D-Ore., recently invited his colleagues to begin a new conversation about reforming the health insurance industry. It’s a conversation worth having. Health insurers have grown bigger, more powerful and more deeply embedded in our healthcare system than ever before—to the detriment of patients and taxpayers alike. But if Democrats are serious about […]

The post Democrats Didn’t Discover The Insurance Crisis. They Created It appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Test-Score Growth Is the Best Metric We Have for Understanding School Performance
item.description:
We’ve written a lot at the Show-Me Institute lately about A–F letter grades for public schools. The Missouri Department of Elementary and Secondary Education (DESE) will soon begin assigning these […]

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Connecticut AI Bill Would Put Workplace Technology on the Bargaining Table
item.description:
A bill headed to the Connecticut Senate floor would reshape how employers use artificial intelligence (AI) in the workplace — adding new regulatory requirements, compliance costs, and, in the public […]

The post Connecticut AI Bill Would Put Workplace Technology on the Bargaining Table  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Conversation continues at the State Capitol about K-3 suspensions
item.description:
The 2023 ban on suspensions (exclusionary discipline) for K-3 students continues to invite commentary. Conversation erupted once more last week during the House Education Finance Committee meeting. Rep. Bryan Lawrence brought forth HF 3493, which, among other things, primarily broadened the list of schools available for safe schools funding. An amendment attached to the bill...

The post Conversation continues at the State Capitol about K-3 suspensions appeared first on American Experiment.

item.description:
I wrote recently that in the lead up to the public vote, even earnings tax defenders could not defend the earnings tax. Despite urging yes votes, they conceded many, if […]

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Massachusetts ‘millionaires tax’ cost the state $4 billion in departing income
item.description:
The vast majority of that loss came from high-income earners leaving the state.

The post Massachusetts ‘millionaires tax’ cost the state $4 billion in departing income appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Even open enrollment opponents say it will benefit students
item.description:
The debate over public school open enrollment is over. How do we know? Opponents have conceded the main point: Open enrollment benefits students.  Senate Bill 101, arguably the most important bill of the 2026 legislative session, would let students enroll in any New Hampshire public school that has room for them. Have a problem with […]

The post Even open enrollment opponents say it will benefit students appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] False statement
item.description:
Abdullahi Mohamud Mohamed was arraigned today in federal court in downtown Minneapolis on one count of false statement. The April 7 indictment (26-cv-78) reads, as follows, and, I’ll go out on a limb and say that there may be more to this story. Also charged today in federal court was Said Jama Ahmed, accused of...

The post False statement appeared first on American Experiment.
